title: SCEMQA 子题 1
rewritten_question_text:
Based on the boxplots shown, is Statement I true or false?
Statement I: The range of the math scores equals the range of the verbal scores.

title: SCEMQA 子题 2
rewritten_question_text:
Based on the boxplots shown, is Statement II true or false?
Statement II: The highest math score equals the median verbal score.

title: SCEMQA 子题 3
rewritten_question_text:
Based on the boxplots shown, is Statement III true or false?
Statement III: The verbal scores appear to be roughly symmetric, while the math scores appear to be skewed to the right.

title: SCEMQA 开放题
rewritten_question_text: The prices, in thousands of dollars, of 304 homes recently sold in a city are summarized in the histogram below. Based on the histogram, what statement must be true?
expected_answer: The median price is not greater than $750,000.

title: SCEMQA 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As part of a study on the relationship between the use of tanning booths and the occurrence of skin cancer, researchers reviewed the medical records of 1,436 people. The table below summarizes tanning booth use for people in the study who did and did not have skin cancer. Of the people in the study who had skin cancer, what fraction used a tanning booth?

title: SCEMQA 子题 1
rewritten_question_text: In northwest Pennsylvania, a zoologist recorded the ages, in months, of 55 bears and whether each bear was male or female. The data are shown in the back-to-back stemplot below. Based on the stemplot, how does the median age of female bears compare with the median age of male bears?
expected_answer: The median age is greater for female bears than for male bears.

title: SCEMQA 子题 2
rewritten_question_text: In northwest Pennsylvania, a zoologist recorded the ages, in months, of 55 bears and whether each bear was male or female. The data are shown in the back-to-back stemplot below. Based on the stemplot, how does the range of ages for female bears compare with the range of ages for male bears?
expected_answer: The range of ages is greater for female bears than for male bears.

title: SCEMQA 开放题
rewritten_question_text:
Nutritionists examined the sodium content of different brands of potato chips. Each brand was classified as either healthy or regular based on how the chips were marketed to the public. The sodium contents, in milligrams (mg) per serving, of the chips are summarized in the boxplots below.

Based on the boxplots, what is the correct comparison between the two classifications of the sodium content of the chips?
expected_answer: The interquartile range (IQR) of the brands classified as healthy is greater than the IQR of the brands classified as regular.

title: Dataset 开放题
rewritten_question_text: A factory has two machines, A and B, making the same part for refrigerators. The number of defective parts produced by each machine during the first hour of operation was recorded on 19 randomly selected days. The scatterplot shows the number of defective parts produced by each machine on the selected days. Based on the scatterplot, what is the best comparison between the number of defective parts produced by the two machines during the first hour of operation on the 19 days?
expected_answer: Machine A usually, but not always, produced fewer defective parts than machine B.

title: SCEMQA 子题 1
rewritten_question_text: A state educational agency was concerned that the salaries of public school teachers in one region of the state, region A, were higher than the salaries in another region of the state, region B. The agency took two independent random samples of salaries of public school teachers, one from region A and one from region B. The data are summarized in the table below. Assuming all conditions for inference are met, do the data provide convincing statistical evidence that the salaries of public school teachers in region A are, on average, greater than the salaries of public school teachers in region B?

title: SCEMQA 子题 2
rewritten_question_text: Using the same data and assuming all conditions for inference are met, identify the significance levels supported by the conclusion. State whether the result is significant at α=0.05 and whether it is significant at α=0.01.
expected_answer: It is significant at α=0.05 but not at α=0.01.

title: SCEMQA 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As part of a community service program, students in three middle school grades (grade 6, grade 7, grade 8) each chose to participate in one of three school-sponsored volunteer activities. The graph below shows the distribution for each class for the three activities. Based on the graph, what must be true for students in grade 7 about the number who chose activity C compared with the number who chose activity B?
expected_answer: For students in grade 7, the number who chose activity C was greater than the number who chose activity B.

title: SCEMQA 子题 1
rewritten_question_text: The number of siblings was recorded for each student in a group of 80 students. Some summary statistics and a histogram displaying the results are shown below. Using the given information, how does the median compare with the mean?
expected_answer: The median is less than the mean.

expected_answer: A line is not an appropriate model to describe the relation between X and Y.

title: SCEMQA 子题 1

请只回答置信区间。

title: SCEMQA 子题 2
rewritten_question_text:
Thirteen large animals were measured to help determine the relationship between their length and their weight. The computer output from the analysis is given below. Interpret the 99% confidence interval for the slope of the regression line.
请只回答区间解释。
expected_answer: We are 99% confident that the true slope of the regression line is between 0.032 and 0.041.

title: SCEMQA 子题 1

title: SCEMQA 开放题
rewritten_question_text: Researchers in the Southwest are studying tortoises, a species of animal affected by habitat loss due to human development of the desert. A total of 78 tortoises are being studied at several sites, and the data on gender and species is organized in the table shown below. If a tortoise from this study is selected at random, let A = the tortoise is female and B = the tortoise is a Morafka. In context, what does P(B|A) represent?
expected_answer: 31.3% is the probability that a randomly selected female tortoise is a Morafka tortoise.

title: SCEMQA 开放题
rewritten_question_text: Breakfast cereals have a wide range of sugar content. Some cereals contain High Fructose Corn Syrup (HFCS) as a source of sugar and some do not. The boxplots show the total sugar content of different types of cereal for those containing HFCS and for those that do not. Based on the boxplots, state one true comparison between the sugar content distributions of cereals with HFCS and cereals without HFCS.
expected_answer: About half the cereals with HFCS have more sugar than about three-fourths of the cereals without HFCS.

title: SCEMQA 子题 1
rewritten_question_text: In the northern United States, schools are sometimes closed during winter due to severe snowstorms. At the end of the school year, schools have to make up for the days missed. Based on this distribution, what measure should be used to describe the center of the distribution?
